reviews.text: Wi-Fi was very limited with low bandwidth. Was charged extra for early check-in on an hourly basis( almost 650₹ extra), which was not informed during booking . The space is small and not comfortable for families or groups. Overall, not a good experience.

reviews.name: Mayur kadam
reviews.text:
I had a very poor experience here and would not recommend this hotel, especially for travelers coming from outside the area.

At the time of arrival, we were clearly told that early check-in was allowed. However, after some time, a staff member came and demanded extra payment for early check-in, which was never mentioned initially. This felt misleading and unprofessional.

The owner’s behavior was also unacceptable. From the beginning, he spoke very rudely and even started shouting when we said we would not pay the unexpected early check-in charge. There was no polite or respectful communication.

Another major issue was the inconsistency between staff and owner — staff members would say one thing, and later the owner would say something completely different. This created confusion and distrust.

Other problems we faced:
- Wi-Fi was not working
- Rooms are built too close to each other, making it difficult to get proper sunlight and natural ventilation.
- Owner were not helpful and very rude

Overall, it felt unsafe and dishonest. I strongly advise other outsiders and travelers to be careful before booking here.

reviews.name: Deepak Jain
reviews.text: This resort perfectly blends adventure and serenity. Enjoy thrilling boat ride, relax by the sparkling pool, and wander through lush plantations. It’s an idyllic, refreshing escape for nature lovers.

reviews.name: Sanjith fabz
reviews.text:
stayed at Kadakani River Resort recently, and it turned out to be a perfect getaway in the heart of Coorg. Surrounded by lush greenery and the soothing sound of the river, the resort offers a calm and refreshing atmosphere that instantly puts you at ease.

The rooms were clean, spacious, and well maintained, with beautiful views that made waking up in the morning truly special. The entire property feels very peaceful and close to nature.

The staff were extremely courteous and helpful throughout my stay, always making sure everything was comfortable. The food was another highlight—delicious, homely, and prepared with great care, giving a lovely taste of authentic local flavors.

Spending time sitting by the river and enjoying the natural surroundings was the most memorable part of my stay. If you’re looking for a relaxing nature retreat with excellent hospitality, Kadakani River Resort is highly recommended. A wonderful place to unwind and reconnect with nature.

title: Porcupine Castle Resort Coorg
description: Relaxed chalets & treetop rooms in an eco-chic resort with an outdoor pool & a casual restaurant.
address: Porcupine Castle Resort Road, Hanchikad,, Pollibetta, Karnataka 571215, India
hotelDescription:
On a 121-hectare coffee plantation overlooking mountains and valleys, this exotic, eco-chic resort is 20 km from the Coorg Golf Links.
 Relaxed, cosy chalets offer free Wi-Fi, flat-screen TVs, minifridges, and tea and coffeemaking equipment. All come with sofabeds, living/dining areas and balconies. Upgraded chalets add mountain or valley views. There are also 2 rustic-chic treetop rooms.
 Freebies include parking and breakfast, which is provided in a casual restaurant serving Asian cuisine. Other amenities consist of an outdoor pool and a kids' playground with a trampoline. Plantation tours are offered for a fee. Pets are welcome.

address: Arji Hills, Near Perumbadi Checkpost, Virajpet, Karnataka 571218, India
hotelDescription:
On 34 hectares of grounds that have a coffee plantation and a nature reserve, this tranquil resort off the NH 91 highway is 16 km from the Brahmagiri Wildlife Sanctuary.
 Featuring antique and upcycled furniture, the rustic cottages come with flat-screen TVs and minibars, as well as tea and coffeemaking facilities. All have living areas, as well as patios offering area views.
 Breakfast is served in an informal restaurant. There's also an alcohol-free bar; guests may bring their own alcohol. Other amenities include sports courts, a game room and a TV lounge. Trekking, plantation tours and bike rentals are offered.

title: WelcomHeritage Fernhills Royal Palace
description: Opulent hotel in a 19th-century palace offering lush gardens, antiques-filled suites & fine dining.
address: Ferrnhills Post, Ooty, Tamil Nadu 643004, India
hotelDescription:
Set in a former palace dating from 1844, this opulent all-suite hotel on 20 hectares of landscaped grounds is 2 km from both Udagamandalam train station (part of the Nilgiri Mountain Railway) and boating at Ooty Lake.
 Stately, antiques-filled suites with period features have living rooms and dressing rooms. All offer fireplaces, whirlpool tubs, flat-screen TVs and minibars, as well as tea and coffeemaking facilities.
 Breakfast is served in the ornate, upscale restaurant. Other amenities include a formal wood-paneled bar and a grand ballroom, as well as lush gardens with sitting areas.

hotelDescription:
Featuring views of the surrounding tea fields, this trendy hostel off the NH85 road is 4 km from Attukad Waterfalls, and 8 km from tours and tastings at Kolukkumalai Tea Estate.
 The simple mixed-gender and female-only dorms provide Wi-Fi, bunk beds and shared bathrooms. Linens are included, and towels can be rented. Private rooms offer en suite bathrooms, and balconies with tea field views.
 Amenities include a restaurant, a colourful guest lounge, and a rooftop terrace with views of the fields. Breakfast and parking are available.

address: 77, Sylks Rd, Monterosa Colony, Ooty, Tamil Nadu 643001, India
hotelDescription:
Set in a series of 19th-century cottages nestled among 2.5 hectares of genteel gardens and rolling hills, this upscale hotel is 4.5 km from the Gymkhana Golf Course and 9 km from Doddabetta mountain.
 The sophisticated rooms and suites, with traditional wood decor, high ceilings and subdued tones, feature balconies, free Wi-Fi, TVs and minibars, as well as coffeemakers; most have fireplaces. Room service is available 24/7.
 Parking and breakfast are complimentary. A lounge offers formal dining; there's also a casual restaurant and an elegant fireside bar, plus a 24-hour gym, an Ayurvedic spa and outdoor sports facilities.

reviews.name: Soumya Mukherjee
reviews.text:
Location Can’t Save a Sinking Ship
If real estate is the only thing that matters to you, Hotel Maurya Bhubaneswar has it in spades. Positioned perfectly for anyone needing to navigate the heart of the city, its location is—quite literally—its only saving grace. Unfortunately, once you step through the front doors, the experience goes downhill faster than a "dropped packet" of data.
While the rooms are decent and well-maintained, they are overshadowed by service standards that feel less like hospitality and more like an endurance test.
The "Service" (Or Lack Thereof)
The room service here can only be described as abysmal. Whether it’s a lack of training or a lack of staff, the result is a frustratingly slow and unresponsive experience. In a city known for its warmth, the service here feels cold and indifferent.
Bizarre Rules & Logistics
The hotel operates under a set of "house rules" that defy modern logic:
The Tea/Coffee Curfew: Good luck getting a caffeine fix in the evening. The hotel has a baffling rule prohibiting tea or coffee service after 5:30 PM.
Early Kitchen Closure: The kitchen shuts down at 9:30 PM, which is remarkably early for a city hotel. If you are arriving late from a long day of sightseeing or work, expect to go hungry or rely on delivery apps.
The Ice-less Bar: In perhaps the most confusing twist of all, the bar—an establishment designed to serve chilled drinks—does not have ice cubes. Trying to enjoy a drink here is a lesson in lukewarm disappointment.
A Groundhog Day Breakfast
While breakfast is included, don't expect variety. The menu repeats identically every single day. By day three, the lack of innovation becomes a chore rather than a meal.
The Verdict
The universe may not have an intent to harm us, but this hotel’s management certainly seems intent on testing your patience.
Pros: * Excellent, central location.
Decent, clean rooms.
Cons: * Frustratingly restricted service hours.
Repetitive dining options.
A general lack of basic amenities (like ice).
Final Word: Stay here only if you absolutely must be in this specific neighborhood and plan on spending every waking hour—including mealtimes—somewhere else.

reviews.name: Mark Hammond
reviews.text: Excellent lodging in a great location in the special little town of Hampi. My room was large, clean, comfortable, and everything worked as it should; the property also has a large patio with outdoor seating for guests to enjoy. The owner is a great guy who will help ensure you have a rewarding stay. It's a short walk to the main temple, and ancient ruins up Hematuka Hill and down the Hampi Bazaar road. Hampi is pretty much no cars allowed, making it easy to stroll through town and relish its unique ambiance. Coffee, tea, and water are complimentary at Kalyan; for breakfast just go across the street or around the corner. I booked two nights initially and extended to a third given the many things to see and do around Hampi, and the pleasant accommodation I enjoyed at Kalyan Guest House. In the pic, Kalyan is on the left... and look at that boulder!

reviews.name: Y U
reviews.text:
Depuis plus de 30 ans je rêvais de venir en Inde du Sud et de passer au moins 24h sur un bateau backwaters au Kérala pour découvrir les canaux, leurs village, la faune.
Mon agent de voyage m’a conseillé de réserver un bateau géré par les hôtels Abad. Pourquoi pas?
Le départ et l’arrivée du bateau ont lieu à l’hôtel Abad whispering palms.
Première déception : à mon arrivée à la réception de l’hôtel, point de départ de la mini croisière, , on m’ explique que la navigation se ferait uniquement de 12h à  17h. Ensuite le bateau serait amarré à l’hôtel, le repas du soir et le petit déjeuner seront servi à bord du bateau.
Je pensais vraiment passer la nuit au milieu du lac ou dans un canal Comme c’est le cas par exemple quand on réserve un bateau privé ven baie d’halong au Vietnam ou l’on passe vraiment 24h sur l’eau.

La promenade sur le lac est peu interessante : on n’y voit ni village ni faune.
Pour voir les canaux et les habitations locales il faut payer un supplément et monter dans une barque à moteur. Le tour de 45mn coûte 1000rp par personne

Même si cela est court cette promenade a sauvé un peu notre journée de la catastrophe, il était bon de voir la population locale vivre au bord de ces canaux. Par contre cela n’a rien de sauvage.

Heureusement notre capitaine qui était particulièrement gentil nous a proposé de rentrer au soleil couchant . À ce moment de la journée nous avons pu apercevoir quelques oiseaux.

Le bateau lui même était assez ancien mais joli extérieurement et intérieurement. Malheureusement la salle de bain était en très mauvais état:le tuyau de douche était cassé , l’eau n’arrivait pas dans le pommeau et l’eau était froide .

Les repas étaient corrects: pas très copieux mais bons.

Au final j’ai trouvé le rapport qualité / prix plutôt mauvais.

Je pense aussi que le sur-tourisme à eu des effets catastrophiques sur la région et ce que l’on peut lire dans les brochures sur l’écho system de cette région me semble  très  loin de la réalité. La région m’a énormément déçue par rapport à ce que j’en attendais

En fait plutôt que le house boat, le meilleur moyen pour découvrir les backwaters et ses lagunes est de louer une pirogue motorisée ou, pour une balade plus sportive, en kayak. Ces deux modes de navigation vous permettent d'accéder aux parties reculées des backwaters et d'être, par conséquent, au plus près de la nature et de la vie des villages qui les bordent. Hélas je ne le savais pas avant de réserver cette péniche
reviews.textTranslated:
For over 30 years I'd dreamed of coming to South India and spending at least 24 hours on a backwater boat in Kerala to explore the canals, their villages, and the wildlife.
My travel agent advised me to book a boat operated by Abad Hotels. Why not?
The boat departs from and arrives at the Abad Whispering Palms hotel.
My first disappointment: upon arriving at the hotel reception, the starting point of the mini-cruise, I was told that the boat trip would only run from 12 pm to 5 pm. After that, the boat would be moored at the hotel, and dinner and breakfast would be served on board.
I really thought I'd be spending the night in the middle of the lake or in a canal, like when you book a private boat in Halong Bay in Vietnam, where you truly spend 24 hours on the water.

The boat trip on the lake was rather uninteresting: you see neither villages nor wildlife. To see the canals and local houses, you have to pay extra and board a motorboat. The 45-minute tour costs 1000 rupees per person.

Even though it was short, this trip salvaged our otherwise disastrous day. It was nice to see the local people living along the canals. However, it wasn't exactly wild.

Fortunately, our captain, who was particularly kind, offered to take us back at sunset. At that time of day, we were able to spot some birds.

The boat itself was quite old but attractive both inside and out. Unfortunately, the bathroom was in very poor condition: the shower hose was broken, the water didn't reach the showerhead, and the water was cold.

The meals were decent: not very substantial, but tasty.

In the end, I found the value for money rather poor.

I also think that overtourism has had catastrophic effects on the region, and what you read in brochures about the region's ecosystem seems very far from reality. The region disappointed me enormously compared to my expectations.

In fact, rather than a houseboat, the best way to discover the backwaters and their lagoons is to rent a motorized dugout canoe or, for a more adventurous trip, a kayak. These two modes of transport allow you to access the remote parts of the backwaters and, consequently, to be as close as possible to nature and the life of the villages that line them. Unfortunately, I didn't know this before booking the houseboat.

reviews.name: D S
reviews.text:
We travelled as a family with two kids and stayed at Abad Cruise – Houseboat on 1st October 2025, booking the one-bedroom private houseboat for our family.

One of the key advantages of choosing this houseboat was that it’s linked to Abad Whispering Palms Resort, Kumarakom, which allowed us to park our car safely at the resort and use its facilities like the swimming pool and play area when the boat docked in the evening — a major plus point for families with kids.

Everything was well-organized from the start. The staff shared the meal plan and cruise schedule in advance, including the exact route and timings. We were welcomed at the resort with refreshing drinks and guided to the jetty for boarding.

Once on board, the excitement was palpable, especially for the kids. The houseboat was clean and comfortable, with AC in both the bedroom and living area. The bed was cozy, ensuring a good night’s rest after a long day. The TV wasn’t used, and the only minor issue was the poor mobile network connectivity, which is quite expected in the backwaters.

The onboard staff were friendly and professional, especially Captain Amal Dev, who stood out for his politeness and warm interactions. He even allowed our kids to “be the captain” for a few minutes, which absolutely thrilled them!

The food cooked onboard was delightful — a highlight of the trip. Authentic Kerala flavors - particularly the fish fry was simply delicious. In the evening, we were served pazham pori and tea, adding a perfect local touch.

The cruise through the Vembanad Lake was serene and scenic, passing paddy fields, village homes, and flocks of cormorants. Around 2:30 pm, the boat stopped for the staff’s lunch break, during which we took a canoe ride through narrow canals. It was a beautiful experience witnessing lakeside village life and even spotting floating shops selling tender coconuts and ice cream — giving off charming Vietnam/Thailand vibes.

Our houseboat sailed until 5:00 pm, with the option to extend till 6:00 pm instead of doing the morning ride. We opted for the evening extension, during which we stopped by a local fish stall near the paddy fields to buy fresh catch for dinner.

By 6:00 pm, the boat docked back at Abad Whispering Palms Resort, where we spent the evening enjoying the swimming pool, play area, and indoor games like table tennis and badminton. To top it off, there was a live classical music concert at the resort — a soothing end to a memorable day.

Dinner back on the boat was fantastic, especially the freshly cooked fish. The next morning, however, the only downside was the staff’s insistence on an early checkout, repeatedly prompting us from 7 am despite the official time being 9 am. This was quite unnecessary and slightly soured an otherwise perfect experience.

Overall, Abad Cruise – Houseboat offered a wonderful mix of comfort, hospitality, and authentic Kerala backwater charm. Our kids loved every bit of it, and the tie-up with Abad Whispering Palms made the stay even more enjoyable. Highly recommended for families looking for a peaceful and memorable backwater experience in Kumarakom!

reviews.text: Had the most amazing experience at Swaswara. For the first time, we did a wellness retreat and it was the best! Staff was very warm, friendly and professional. Food was great, there were many wellness activities to do and the resort itself was beyond beautiful. Beach was just 5 mins away, swimming pool was pristine, rooms were so comfortable. The staff ensured the stay was absolutely wonderful at every point. Would definitely visit again :)

reviewContext.Hotel highlights: Luxury, Great view, Quiet, Great value

reviews.name: Lindsey Angisetty
reviews.text:
My husband and I had a fantastic week-long stay at SwaSwara. The villa was beautiful - we loved the open roof concept for maximum fresh air (bedroom and bathroom, except shower, are enclosed). Taking a shower in the open air with the purified rain water was a healing experience in itself. My hair and skin feel so nice after one week without chemicals and harsh water. All the amenities one needs are included, including a hair dryer.

The overall location and surroundings is ideal for wellness. My favorite place on the grounds was the Meditation Hill which overlooks Om Beach and is a serene oasis for yoga, meditation or just chilling and watching the birds and beach action. Lots of fun nature watching anywhere on the grounds (non-people bothering monkeys and lots of cute squirrels, frogs, birds and even a peacock).

The food is perfect for my palate - healthy, mild spice (you can specify mild, med, spicy for the general stay) and vegetarian plus fish. I generally only eat two meals a day at home but didn’t want to miss anything the chefs lovingly prepared with the veggies from the garden on the property, so I ended up eating quite a bit (for me) without gaining any weight and feeling great. The chefs are incredibly accommodating for any food allergies and preferences.

My husband and I had consultations with the Ayurvedic doctors at the beginning of the trip (even though we weren’t doing the specific Ayurvedic program) and received a custom massage treatment plan for the week. The massages were very relaxing and rejuvenating - thoroughly enjoyed them. The doctors were very helpful and caring.

I felt all of the staff went above and beyond to make sure we were happy and well taken care of. With only 24 villas, it was never busy or crowded, but there were also activities that brought people together if you want to meet people and make a few new friends. Would highly recommend joining a cooking class and making some pottery in the art studio, of course in addition to any yoga or meditation session.

Next time, I would like to try the Ayurvedic program or even the 21 day Panchakarma program which is more strict with the food and treatment options and also activities you can do (no swimming or exploring the town). I spoke with several people who had done these programs, and they said they felt completely rejuvenated (and their faces were literally glowing!) This is a gem of a place. Yes, the price tag is certainly higher than other Ayurvedic centers, but it felt more like a vacation than a hospital stay and that makes the extra cost worth it. We will definitely be back!

reviews.name: Shalini Roy
reviews.text:
Hii. I am Shalini Roy. My Sister-in-law and I stayed in the property from 1st February to 5th February. It was overall a good stay. The staffs were helpful and they arranged whatever we needed. It was a safe stay. But there were some issues we want to mention specifically. First of all the property was not well maintained. It was not clean and not like the other property of Red Earth. Second when we gave them our room keys for cleaning when we were out, I found my bracelet was missing after returning. I informed them then and there and after 15-20 mins they came with the bracelet and informed me that by mistakenly they took it. I didn't expect that. And because it had elastic, I found that it got loose. N third when we asked for one particular person as our airport drop driver, first they told that they will inform but later they neither assign him nor inform us. Highly dissatisfied with this behaviour.
But overall whenever we asked for any help or room service they were there. The food was good and the other services were fine. Thank you!
reviews.responseFromOwnerText:
Thank you for taking the time to share your detailed feedback, Shalini. We're genuinely glad your stay felt safe, that the team was helpful when you needed them, and that you enjoyed the food.

That said, we sincerely regret the concerns you've raised. On the matter of your bracelet - it was inadvertently picked up along with the bed linen and sent to the laundry. The moment this was noticed, it was returned to you immediately. We understand how unsettling that must have felt, and our team on the ground is deeply apologetic. We have since had a firm conversation with the housekeeping staff to ensure all linen is carefully checked before being moved to the laundry room - this should not happen again.

We also take note of your feedback regarding the upkeep and cleanliness of the property, as well as the miscommunication around your airport transfer request. These are not the standards we hold ourselves to, and we are addressing both with the team directly.

Your trust means a great deal to us, Shalini. Feedback like yours - specific and honest - is what helps us get better. We truly hope to have the chance to welcome you and your sister-in-law back and give you the experience that reflects the best of Red Earth Gokarna.

Team Red Earth

reviews.name: KOMAL JADHAV
reviews.text:
We stayed at Hotel Mayura Sudarshan from 20th to 23rd February, and our experience was extremely disappointing.

Before booking through MMT, we specifically called the hotel reception and clearly requested Rooms 4, 5, or 6, as we had seen them online and they suited our family’s needs. The staff confirmed our request over the phone, and based on that assurance, we proceeded with the booking.

However, upon arrival, we were allotted completely different rooms. The reason given was that the hotel was “full.” Shockingly, during our entire 3-night stay, only one other room appeared to be occupied. This explanation did not seem genuine.

We repeatedly informed the reception that we were traveling with small children and had specifically enquired in advance about those particular rooms. Despite this, our request was outright refused.

The room given to us was not clean.

Towels provided had visible stains.

Walls had noticeable seepage issues.

Overall hygiene and maintenance were poor.

When we expressed our dissatisfaction and mentioned that we had somehow adjusted for two nights but wanted to cancel the third night with a refund, the staff’s response was extremely unprofessional. The bearded man at reception (possibly named Sadanand) was arrogant and dismissive. Instead of addressing our concern, he simply said, “If you want to go, checkout is at 11,” and walked away while we were still speaking. Completely unacceptable behavior.

As for the food — it’s better to eat outside. You will find far better taste and variety elsewhere in Ooty.

The only positive aspect of this hotel is the garden and the view. Unfortunately, that alone cannot compensate for poor service, lack of cleanliness, and unprofessional staff behavior.

Overall, a very bad experience. We would not recommend this hotel to families, especially those traveling with children.

reviews.name: Sally Holbrook
reviews.text:
Not necessarily the physical property itself was my issue, it was other things that unfortunately ruined my days in Hampi. I would like to share that I have travelled a huge amount, to over 90 countries, and it is very rare for me to encounter what I did at Rocky’s. Normally I stay in dorm rooms at hostels, but decided to treat myself in Hampi, which was clearly a mistake. Never before have I been treated so badly.

I also need to mention that the place was extremely noisy. This was not due to guests or the road, but the staff themselves, who were shouting very loudly outside my room early in the morning. It was not occasional noise, it was loud and disruptive.

The main issue however was their Tuk Tuk driver, Eggy. While he may not technically be staff, he is effectively treated as such and clearly works closely with the property. He came and picked me up from the bus, which was no problem. The next day we agreed he would take me on a tour from 9am to 6pm, and we agreed a price of 1,600 rupees. I told him several times that I am very quick at going around places, but he insisted it would take nine hours. I agreed.

At the first temple I came out and could not see him. There were several Tuk Tuks there. He started shouting at me in Hindi to attract my attention, but I cannot understand the language, so I sent him a WhatsApp message. When I finally saw him, after maybe two minutes, he just kept talking at me. When I said I did not understand what he was saying and that I was sorry, he started to mimic me, no kidding, saying things like “I don’t understand Hindi” in a very sarcastic tone. This behaviour continued. Every time I said anything he repeated it back to me in a sarcastic and mimicking way, taking the Mickey out of my accent.

After about an hour of this I was nearly in tears, and I do not cry. I was so humiliated. I finished the whole tour in four hours, yet he still expected to charge me the full amount, even though the distance covered was not far in total. I then asked if we could do another area since I had technically agreed a price for the whole day. I would have been happy to pay the additional fuel cost, but he just shouted at me, called Rocky, and became even angrier.

In the end I paid 1,000 rupees, which was more than half of what we agreed. He was not happy. I got out of the Tuk Tuk and walked the rest of the way.

I would not stay here again and would strongly suggest staying elsewhere. For example, the place directly opposite has fabulous food and great temple views from the rooftop, and would have been a much better choice. It is called Gopi - and the people I spoke to who stayed there loved it! Book there 😊

reviews.name: abi varma
reviews.text:
A Fantastic Stay with a Few Friendly Suggestions.

I recently stayed at Coorg Cliffs Resort, and I must say, the experience was absolutely fantastic and totally worth the price! From the moment we checked in until the very end, the hospitality and service were just awesome. The room and the location are both simply awesome—you really feel the beauty of Coorg here.

The Highlights: What We Loved!

Heartwarming Hospitality: The staff here really knows how to win hearts. Everyone makes sure you are treated well. They are constantly smiling, checking in on you, and speaking to you like a known person—it feels like being among friends. They really make sure you are taken care of!

Dining Service is Simply Superb: Especially the food service—it’s just awesome. Even though it’s a buffet, staff members keep asking what you want and even serve dishes directly from the live counter to your table. That’s really a class apart! The food taste and variety are excellent, which many other resorts miss.

The Infinity Pool – A Total Vibe: Their infinity pool was awesome and we enjoyed it a lot! Since it’s an open-air pool, you might see some leaves or small insects, which is understandable in the hills. However, a little more frequent cleaning would be great since kids use it frequently.

Excellent Guest Management: We noticed the resort handled a private group gathering very professionally. Even though they were enjoying their own drinks, the staff ensured they were organized separately so there was no loud music or disturbance to other guests.

Kid-Friendly & Personal Connection: They connect with kids so well and make them feel right at home. A big plus for us was that most of the staff speak our language, so we could connect so easily.

Activities: The plantation walk was awesome and short—perfect to enjoy the greenery. The kids' park is also very good!

A Friendly Update on Expectations (Suggestions for Management)
We had such a wonderful time that we want to share a few "friendly updates" on things that could make the guest experience even more perfect:

Room Privacy & Comfort (Room 103): Since our room was right by the pool, there was a bit of a loss of privacy with people standing right near the balcony and the sound of shouting from the pool. Also, the spring mattresses caused some body pain at the corners, which made it a bit tough to get a proper sleep.

Bathroom Drainage: We noticed there is no water hole/drain in the toilet area itself. There is only a small pipe connecting the toilet and bath area, but it doesn't allow water to flow out at all. Some water accidentally spilled near the toilet (kids being kids!), and we had to deal with a wet floor the whole time as the water had nowhere to go.

Food Sensitivity: While the food was 10/10 for taste and variety, unfortunately, our whole family had a stomach upset and pain in the middle of the night. I noticed a few other reviews mentioned this too, so it might be something the kitchen team can double-check just to be safe!

Parking Care: Since the parking is open, our pure black car got fully covered in dust, leaves, and bird poop. It’s hard to find a car wash during a vacation, so we had to travel with a dirty car. A covered parking area would be a huge blessing!

Recreation: The outdoor badminton court is tough to use because of the sun and wind in the hills. If you keep this indoor, I bet you will have many more guests enjoying the game!

Small Essentials: We missed having hair conditioner in the toiletries, especially needed after a dip in the swimming pool!

Final Thoughts
If these small items are looked into, this resort will for sure be the most competitive and well-suited place in the area. We had a wonderful stay and will definitely visit again for sure! Thanks to all staffs who gave us a good memories.
